story.title: Meine Geschichte
story.p1: Zwischen 2012 und 2016 entwickelte ich mobile Anwendungen als Teil des dreamBit-Teams. In diesen Jahren hatte ich die Gelegenheit, an verschiedenen Projekten zu arbeiten, die Feinheiten der mobilen Entwicklung zu erlernen und Apps zu erstellen, die Benutzer liebten.
story.p2: Nach einer langen Pause von der aktiven Entwicklung bin ich zurückgekehrt, um meiner Leidenschaft nachzugehen - der Entwicklung mobiler Apps. Diesmal baue ich Apps als Hobby, angetrieben von meiner Liebe zur Technologie und Kreativität.
story.p3: Heute konzentriere ich mich auf die Entwicklung intelligenter und intuitiver mobiler Anwendungen, die Menschen in ihrem täglichen Leben helfen können. Jede App, die ich erstelle, wird mit Aufmerksamkeit für Details, Benutzererfahrung und innovative Lösungen für reale Probleme entwickelt.

items.question: Who is Zuki Apps?
items.answer: Zuki Apps is an independent mobile software publisher. The developer is based in Israel, previously developed Android and iOS apps as part of DreamBit Apps (2012–2016), and now ships consumer apps built with Flutter for iOS and Android.

items.question: What is zukiapps.com?
items.answer: This site is the public marketing presence for Zuki Apps: short descriptions of each app, links to the App Store and Google Play when available, support email, and links to each app’s Privacy Policy and Terms of Use. There is no user login on the website itself.

items.question: How do I contact Zuki Apps?
items.answer: For support and general inquiries, email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com. The same address appears on app pages and in store listings where applicable.

items.question: Where can I read privacy policies and terms?
items.answer: Each app has its own pages: open the app’s section on this site and follow Privacy Policy and Terms of Use. EU Digital Services Act (DSA) trader information is on the DSA Compliance page. A legacy DreamBit archive is also linked from the footer.

items.question: Which apps are listed on this site?
items.answer: The home page showcases multiple titles (for example ZuList, Hush Gallery, games, utilities, Track Ledger, Noise Meter — Shusher, Paratrooper Blitz, and others). Use the app tiles to open each product page for details and policies.

items.question: Is there a public API documented here?
items.answer: No. This website does not document a public REST or GraphQL API for end users. Apps use their own clients on the device and normal app-store distribution.

section1.title: 1. Informationen, die wir sammeln
section1.content: Wir sammeln Informationen, die Sie uns direkt zur Verfügung stellen, z. B. wenn Sie ein Konto erstellen, unsere Dienste nutzen oder uns für Support kontaktieren.

section2.title: 2. Wie wir Ihre Informationen verwenden
section2.content: Wir verwenden die von uns gesammelten Informationen, um unsere Dienste bereitzustellen, zu warten und zu verbessern, Transaktionen zu verarbeiten und mit Ihnen zu kommunizieren.

section3.title: 3. Informationsweitergabe
section3.content: Wir verkaufen, handeln oder übertragen Ihre persönlichen Informationen nicht ohne Ihre Zustimmung an Dritte, außer wie in dieser Richtlinie beschrieben.

section4.title: 4. Datensicherheit
section4.content: Wir implementieren angemessene Sicherheitsmaßnahmen, um Ihre persönlichen Informationen vor unbefugtem Zugriff, Änderung, Offenlegung oder Zerstörung zu schützen.

section5.title: 5. Ihre Rechte
section5.content: Sie haben das Recht, auf Ihre persönlichen Informationen zuzugreifen, sie zu aktualisieren oder zu löschen. Sie können sich auch von bestimmten Kommunikationen von uns abmelden.

section6.title: 6. Kamera- und Fotobibliothekszugriff
section6.content: ZuList benötigt Zugriff auf die Kamera und Fotobibliothek Ihres Geräts, damit Sie Fotos von Einkaufslistenartikeln aufnehmen und hochladen können. Wir greifen auf diese Funktionen nur zu, wenn Sie explizit wählen, ein Foto zu einer Liste oder einem Artikel hinzuzufügen. Fotos werden sicher in unserem Cloud-Speicher gespeichert und sind nur für Sie und Benutzer zugänglich, mit denen Sie Ihre Listen teilen.

section7.title: 7. Absturzberichterstattung und Fehleranalyse
section7.content: Wir verwenden Firebase Crashlytics, einen von Google bereitgestellten Dienst, um Informationen über App-Abstürze und Fehler zu sammeln. Dies hilft uns, Probleme zu identifizieren und zu beheben, um Ihre Erfahrung zu verbessern.
dataCollectedItems: Technische Informationen über Abstürze und Fehler (Stack-Traces, Fehlermeldungen)
Installations-ID (eine eindeutige Kennung für Ihre App-Installation, nicht mit Ihrer persönlichen Identität verknüpft)
Benutzerdefinierte Diagnoseinformationen im Zusammenhang mit Fehlern (Fehlercodes, Vorgangstypen)
purposeItems: Fehler und Abstürze identifizieren und beheben
section7.dataSharingContent: Diese Daten werden von Google Firebase verarbeitet und unterliegen der Datenschutzerklärung von Google.
section7.dataRetentionContent: Absturzberichte werden von Firebase bis zu 90 Tage aufbewahrt und dann automatisch gelöscht.
section7.yourRightsContent: Sie können sich von der Absturzberichterstattung abmelden, indem Sie sie in Ihren Geräteeinstellungen deaktivieren, obwohl dies uns daran hindern kann, Probleme zu beheben, die Sie betreffen.

section8.title: 8. Kontaktieren Sie uns
section8.content: Wenn Sie Fragen zu dieser Datenschutzerklärung haben, kontaktieren Sie uns bitte unter:

terms.title: Nutzungsbedingungen
terms.intro: Bitte lesen Sie diese Nutzungsbedingungen sorgfältig durch, bevor Sie ZuList verwenden. Durch den Zugriff auf oder die Nutzung unseres Dienstes stimmen Sie diesen Bedingungen zu. Wenn Sie diesen Bedingungen nicht zustimmen, verwenden Sie bitte unseren Dienst nicht.

Durch das Herunterladen, Installieren, Zugreifen auf oder Verwenden des Dienstes stimmen Sie diesen Bedingungen und unserer Datenschutzerklärung zu.
section1.content2: Wenn Sie den Dienst im Namen einer Organisation verwenden, erklären und garantieren Sie, dass Sie die Befugnis haben, diese Organisation an diese Bedingungen zu binden.

section2.title: 2. Beschreibung des Dienstes
section2.content: ZuList ist eine mobile Anwendung, die es Benutzern ermöglicht, Einkaufslisten mit Familie und Freunden zu erstellen, zu verwalten und zu teilen. Der Dienst umfasst:

items.item2: Teilen von Listen mit anderen Benutzern

section3.title: 3. Benutzerkonten und Registrierung
section3.content1: Um bestimmte Funktionen des Dienstes zu nutzen, müssen Sie ein Konto erstellen. Sie können ein Konto erstellen mit:

items2.item2: Alle Aktivitäten, die unter Ihrem Konto stattfinden

section3.content3: Sie müssen mindestens 13 Jahre alt sein, um den Dienst zu nutzen. Wenn Sie unter 18 Jahre alt sind, erklären Sie, dass Sie die Zustimmung eines Elternteils oder Erziehungsberechtigten zur Nutzung des Dienstes erhalten haben. Wenn Sie ein Elternteil oder Erziehungsberechtigter sind und glauben, dass Ihr Kind uns persönliche Informationen zur Verfügung gestellt hat, kontaktieren Sie uns bitte sofort.

section4.content1: ZuList bietet Premium-Abonnementpläne, die Zugang zu zusätzlichen Funktionen bieten:

section4.content2: Mit dem Kauf eines Abonnements stimmen Sie zu:

items2.item3: Preise können je nach Region variieren und können mit Vorankündigung geändert werden

section4.content3: Sie können Ihr Abonnement, einschließlich Kündigung, verwalten über:

items3.item2: Android: Google Play Store → Abonnements → ZuList

section4.content3b: Wenn Sie Ihr Abonnement kündigen, haben Sie weiterhin Zugang zu Premium-Funktionen bis zum Ende Ihrer aktuellen Abrechnungsperiode. Danach wird Ihr Konto auf die kostenlose Stufe zurückgesetzt.
section4.content4: Rückerstattungsanfragen werden von Apple (für iOS) oder Google (für Android) gemäß ihren jeweiligen Rückerstattungsrichtlinien bearbeitet. Wir bearbeiten Rückerstattungen nicht direkt. Für Abonnement-Rückerstattungen kontaktieren Sie bitte den Apple Support oder Google Play Support.
section4.content5: Wenn wir eine kostenlose Testversion anbieten, wird dies zum Zeitpunkt der Anmeldung klar angezeigt. Am Ende der Testphase wird Ihr Abonnement automatisch in ein kostenpflichtiges Abonnement umgewandelt, es sei denn, Sie kündigen vor Ende der Testphase.

privacy.description: Erfahren Sie, wie wir Ihre Daten schützen

q1.question: Wie teile ich eine Einkaufsliste mit Familienmitgliedern?
q1.answer: Um eine Liste zu teilen, öffnen Sie die Liste und tippen Sie auf die Schaltfläche 'Teilen' oder 'Mitglieder'. Sie können Mitglieder per E-Mail, SMS, WhatsApp oder durch Teilen eines Links einladen. Sobald sie die Einladung annehmen, haben sie Zugriff auf die Liste und können Einträge in Echtzeit hinzufügen oder ändern.

q2.question: Wie funktioniert das Premium-Abonnement?
q2.answer: Premium bietet unbegrenzte Listen, unbegrenzte Artikel, unbegrenzte Mitglieder, werbefreie Erfahrung, erweiterte Statistiken, benutzerdefinierte Themes, Offline-Modus und Cloud-Backup. Sie können monatlich oder jährlich über den App Store (iOS) oder Google Play (Android) abonnieren. Sie können Ihr Abonnement jederzeit über die Abonnementeinstellungen Ihres Geräts verwalten oder kündigen.

q3.question: Kann ich ZuList offline verwenden?
q3.answer: Ja! ZuList funktioniert offline. Sie können Listen erstellen, Artikel hinzufügen und Artikel als gekauft markieren, auch ohne Internetverbindung. Alle Änderungen werden automatisch synchronisiert, wenn Sie wieder online sind. Hinweis: Der Offline-Modus ist eine Premium-Funktion.

q4.question: Wie lösche ich mein Konto?
q4.answer: Um Ihr Konto zu löschen, gehen Sie zu Profil → Einstellungen → Konto löschen. Bitte beachten Sie, dass die Kontolöschung dauerhaft ist und nicht rückgängig gemacht werden kann. Alle Ihre Daten, einschließlich Listen, Artikel und Fotos, werden dauerhaft gelöscht. Für detaillierte Anweisungen besuchen Sie unsere Seite zur Kontolöschung.

q5.question: Ich habe Probleme mit Benachrichtigungen. Was soll ich tun?
q5.answer: Stellen Sie zunächst sicher, dass Benachrichtigungen in den Geräteeinstellungen für ZuList aktiviert sind. Wenn Benachrichtigungen immer noch nicht funktionieren, versuchen Sie: 1) Die App neu zu starten, 2) Die Benachrichtigungseinstellungen Ihres Geräts zu überprüfen, 3) Sicherzustellen, dass Sie eine aktive Internetverbindung haben, 4) Die App neu zu installieren, wenn das Problem weiterhin besteht. Wenn die Probleme weiterhin bestehen, kontaktieren Sie uns bitte.

q6.question: Wie stelle ich meine Daten wieder her, wenn ich das Gerät wechsle?
q6.answer: Wenn Sie dasselbe Konto verwenden (Google oder Apple Sign-In), werden Ihre Daten automatisch synchronisiert, wenn Sie sich auf einem neuen Gerät anmelden. Stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie mit dem Internet verbunden sind und sich mit demselben Konto anmelden. Premium-Benutzer haben Cloud-Backup aktiviert, sodass alle Daten sicher gespeichert und wiederhergestellt werden können.

additionalHelp.title: Benötigen Sie weitere Hilfe?
additionalHelp.description: Wenn Sie die gesuchte Antwort nicht finden konnten oder ein technisches Problem haben, zögern Sie bitte nicht, uns zu kontaktieren. Wir sind hier, um zu helfen und antworten normalerweise innerhalb von 24-48 Stunden.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about features, privacy, and billing.

items.question: What is ZuList?
items.answer: A collaborative shopping list app. Create lists, share with others, and sync in real time on iOS and Android.

items.question: Is ZuList free?
items.answer: Yes. Free tier includes up to 5 lists, 150 items per list, and sharing with one other person per list. Premium removes limits and ads.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: You can start as a guest. Sign in to sync across devices and accept invites reliably.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: English, Hebrew, Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Chinese (Simplified), Japanese, and Korean.

items.question: How fast is sync?
items.answer: Usually instant when online. Offline changes upload when connectivity returns.

items.question: Can two people edit at the same time?
items.answer: Yes. Last write wins for the same field; the list stays consistent.

items.question: How do I remove someone from a list?
items.answer: List owner → Members → remove. They lose access immediately.

items.question: Why doesn’t the widget match the list I’m viewing?
items.answer: The widget always shows your **primary list** from Settings.

items.question: How do I add the widget?
items.answer: See [How to: Home screen widget](./how-to-home-widget.md).

items.question: What does Premium include?
items.answer: Unlimited lists, items, and members; no ads; advanced statistics; themes; premium offline features; cloud backup.

items.question: How do I cancel?
items.answer: Manage subscription in the App Store or Google Play—ZuList updates automatically via store webhooks.

items.question: Where is my data stored?
items.answer: Firebase (Google Cloud). Lists are private to you and invited members.

section1.title: 1. Informationen, die wir sammeln
section1.content: Hush Gallery ist mit Datenschutz als Kernprinzip entwickelt. Wir sammeln nur die für die Bereitstellung des Dienstes erforderlichen Informationen. Wir sammeln Informationen, die Sie uns direkt zur Verfügung stellen, z. B. wenn Sie ein Konto erstellen (bei Verwendung von Cloud-Backup), unsere Dienste nutzen oder uns für Support kontaktieren.

section2.title: 2. Wie wir Ihre Informationen verwenden
section2.content: Wir verwenden die von uns gesammelten Informationen, um unsere Dienste bereitzustellen, zu warten und zu verbessern. Ihre Fotos und Videos werden standardmäßig lokal auf Ihrem Gerät gespeichert. Wenn Sie sich für Cloud-Backup entscheiden, werden Ihre Medien clientseitig verschlüsselt, bevor sie gespeichert werden.

section3.title: 3. Informationsweitergabe
section3.content: Wir verkaufen, handeln oder übertragen Ihre persönlichen Informationen oder Medien nicht ohne Ihre Zustimmung an Dritte, außer wie in dieser Richtlinie beschrieben. Ihre Fotos und Videos bleiben privat und sind nur für Sie zugänglich.

section4.title: 4. Datensicherheit
section4.content: Wir implementieren angemessene Sicherheitsmaßnahmen zum Schutz Ihrer persönlichen Informationen und Medien. Alle Cloud-Backups werden clientseitig verschlüsselt, bevor sie übertragen werden. Die lokale Speicherung verwendet das private Verzeichnis der App, auf das andere Apps nicht zugreifen können.

section5.title: 5. Ihre Rechte
section5.content: Sie haben das Recht, jederzeit auf Ihre persönlichen Informationen und Medien zuzugreifen, sie zu aktualisieren oder zu löschen. Sie können Ihr Konto und alle zugehörigen Daten über die App-Einstellungen löschen. Sie können sich auch jederzeit von Cloud-Backup abmelden.

Wir greifen nur auf Ihre Medien zu, wenn Sie explizit den Import wählen. Alle importierten Medien werden im privaten Verzeichnis der App gespeichert und nicht an Dritte weitergegeben.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers for users and search assistants about Hush Gallery features, privacy, and troubleshooting.

items.question: What is Hush Gallery?
items.answer: Hush Gallery is a private photo and video gallery for iOS and Android. Media is stored in the app's private storage (separate from your public Photos app), organized with tags, protected with optional Premium app lock (PIN/biometric), and optionally backed up to iCloud (iOS) or Google Drive (Android). Package: com.zuki.apps.hushGallery.

items.question: How do I import photos and videos into Hush Gallery?
items.answer: Open Hush Gallery and tap the + (Add) button. Choose Import from Gallery, Take Photo, or Take Video. Select one or more items from your device library. After import you can optionally delete the originals from your public gallery. You can also share directly from WhatsApp, Messages, Photos, or other apps using Share → Hush Gallery.

items.question: How does Share to Hush Gallery work on iPhone?
items.answer: In Photos, WhatsApp, or any app: select media → Share → Hush Gallery. The iOS Share Extension copies files to secure app storage and opens Hush Gallery. On first launch after sharing, accept the privacy notice if shown, then confirm import. You may be asked whether to delete originals from the Photos library.

items.question: How does Share to Hush Gallery work on Android?
items.answer: Select photos or videos in any app → Share → Hush Gallery. The app receives the shared files and imports them into private storage. Grant photo access when prompted. Optional: delete originals after import via the in-app dialog or Settings → Deletion → Auto-Delete Source Files.

items.question: Can I delete originals from my public gallery after import?
items.answer: Yes. After each import, Hush Gallery can ask: Delete original files from your photo gallery? Enable Settings → Deletion → Auto-Delete Source Files to skip the prompt. Deletion uses Photos library asset IDs on iOS when available; if identification fails, you will be asked to delete manually for safety.

items.question: How do tags work in Hush Gallery?
items.answer: Create custom tags from the tags bar (Manage Tags). Filter the grid with All, Untagged, or any tag. In selection mode, assign tags to multiple items at once. Free users: up to 5 tags. Premium: unlimited tags. Reorder tags in edit mode.

items.question: What is the difference between Free and Premium?
items.answer: Free: local private gallery, import/export, up to 5 tags, banner ads. Premium (one-time purchase): remove all ads, unlimited tags, App Lock (PIN/biometric/auto-lock), cloud backup, and future premium features. Watch a rewarded ad for 24 hours ad-free on the free tier.

items.question: How do I set up App Lock?
items.answer: Premium required. Go to Settings → Security → App Lock. Set a 4- or 6-digit PIN and optionally enable Face ID / Touch ID / fingerprint. Configure auto-lock timeout (immediately, 1, 5, or 15 minutes). Verified email is required for PIN recovery.

items.question: I forgot my PIN. How do I recover access?
items.answer: On the lock screen tap Forgot PIN. Sign in with the verified email used for recovery, or restore your PIN from an encrypted cloud backup if enabled. Clearing lock settings removes the PIN but does not delete your media.

items.question: How does cloud backup work?
items.answer: Premium feature. Settings → Backup → Backup Now or enable Auto Backup. iOS uses iCloud; Android uses Google Drive. Restore on a new device from Settings or during onboarding. Keep the app open during backup/restore.

items.question: Is my media encrypted?
items.answer: Media is stored in the app's private directory inaccessible to other apps. Client-side encryption for local files and cloud backup is Coming Soon in Settings. Cloud backup uploads to your own iCloud/Google account when enabled.

items.question: Where are my photos stored? Can other apps see them?
items.answer: Imported files live in Hush Gallery's private app storage on your device—not in the public camera roll unless you export them. Other apps cannot browse the vault without your unlock PIN/biometric (Premium).

items.question: How do I export photos back to my device gallery?
items.answer: Open a photo/video → Export to Gallery, or select multiple items in selection mode → Export. Metadata is preserved where supported. Move to Gallery exports and optionally removes the copy from Hush Gallery.

items.question: How does swipe-to-delete work?
items.answer: Swipe a thumbnail left to delete. A snackbar appears for 5 seconds with Undo. Confirm deletes permanently from Hush Gallery (not from the public gallery unless you imported with delete-original).

items.question: What languages does Hush Gallery support?
items.answer: 12+ languages including English, Hebrew, Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese. Change in Settings → Language.

items.question: What is the first-launch privacy notice?
items.answer: On first open, Hush Gallery shows a privacy notice explaining that media stays on your device and changes can be permanent. Tap Continue after accepting, or Not now to defer (shown again next launch).

items.question: How do I delete my account?
items.answer: Settings → Account → Delete Account. See https://zukiapps.com/hush-gallery/delete-account for step-by-step instructions.

items.question: What happens if I uninstall the app?
items.answer: Local private storage is removed with the app unless you exported files first. If cloud backup was enabled, reinstall, sign in, and use Restore from Backup.

items.question: Does Hush Gallery show ads?
items.answer: Free tier shows banner ads and occasional interstitials. Premium removes all ads. Free users can watch a rewarded ad for 24 hours without ads.

items.question: How do I contact support?
items.answer: Email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com or visit https://zukiapps.com/hush-gallery/support. Privacy: https://zukiapps.com/hush-gallery/privacy. Terms: https://zukiapps.com/hush-gallery/terms.

q1.question: Wie importiere ich Fotos und Videos in Hush Gallery?
q1.answer: Um Medien zu importieren, öffnen Sie die App und tippen Sie auf die Schaltfläche '+' oder 'Importieren'. Sie können Fotos und Videos aus der Galerie Ihres Geräts auswählen. Die importierten Medien werden sicher im privaten Verzeichnis der App gespeichert und sind für andere Apps nicht zugänglich.

q2.question: Wie funktioniert Cloud-Backup?
q2.answer: Cloud-Backup ist eine optionale Premium-Funktion. Wenn aktiviert, werden Ihre Fotos und Videos auf Ihrem Gerät verschlüsselt, bevor sie zu iCloud (iOS) oder Google Drive (Android) hochgeladen werden. Die Verschlüsselung erfolgt clientseitig, was bedeutet, dass wir nicht auf Ihre Medien zugreifen können. Sie können Cloud-Backup jederzeit in den Einstellungen aktivieren oder deaktivieren.

q3.question: Wie richte ich die App-Sperre ein?
q3.answer: Gehen Sie zu Einstellungen → Sicherheit → App-Sperre. Sie können eine PIN, ein Passwort einrichten oder die biometrische Authentifizierung (Face ID/Touch ID) aktivieren. Die App wird automatisch nach einer Zeit der Inaktivität gesperrt, die Sie in den Einstellungen konfigurieren können.

q4.question: Wie lösche ich mein Konto?
q4.answer: Um Ihr Konto zu löschen, gehen Sie zu Einstellungen → Konto → Konto löschen. Bitte beachten Sie, dass die Kontolöschung dauerhaft ist und nicht rückgängig gemacht werden kann. Alle Ihre Daten, einschließlich Fotos, Videos, Ordner und Einstellungen, werden dauerhaft gelöscht. Für detaillierte Anweisungen besuchen Sie unsere Seite zur Kontolöschung.

q5.question: Kann ich Hush Gallery ohne Cloud-Backup verwenden?
q5.answer: Ja! Hush Gallery funktioniert vollständig offline. Alle Ihre Fotos und Videos werden standardmäßig lokal auf Ihrem Gerät gespeichert. Cloud-Backup ist optional und wird nur aktiviert, wenn Sie es verwenden möchten. Ihre Medien bleiben privat und sicher, auch ohne Cloud-Backup.

q6.question: Was passiert mit meinen Fotos, wenn ich die App deinstalliere?
q6.answer: Wenn Sie die App deinstallieren, ohne zuvor Ihr Konto zu löschen, werden Ihre lokalen Daten gelöscht. Wenn Sie Cloud-Backup aktiviert hatten, können Sie Ihre Daten wiederherstellen, indem Sie die App neu installieren und sich mit demselben Konto anmelden. Wir empfehlen, wichtige Fotos zu exportieren, bevor Sie die App deinstallieren, wenn Sie kein Cloud-Backup aktiviert haben.

q1.question: How does whistle detection work?
q1.answer: Whistle Camera uses advanced audio processing to detect whistle sounds in real-time. When a whistle is detected, the app can automatically capture a photo or start/stop video recording. You can adjust the sensitivity in the app settings to match your environment.

q2.question: What is incognito mode?
q2.answer: Incognito mode displays a black screen overlay while recording or capturing photos, providing privacy protection. This allows you to capture media discreetly without the screen showing what's being captured.

q3.question: How do I access the camera from the Lock Screen?
q3.answer: Whistle Camera supports quick access from the Lock Screen, Control Center, and Action Button (iOS 18+). Simply add the camera widget to your Control Center or use the Action Button if your device supports it. The app will launch directly into camera mode.

q4.question: Does Whistle Camera require an internet connection?
q4.answer: No, Whistle Camera works completely offline. All photos and videos are stored locally on your device. The app only requires internet access if you choose to share media or if you contact support.

q5.question: How do I adjust whistle detection sensitivity?
q5.answer: Go to Settings → Whistle Detection → Sensitivity. You can adjust the sensitivity slider to make detection more or less sensitive. Higher sensitivity will detect quieter whistles, while lower sensitivity requires louder whistles.

q6.question: Can I use Whistle Camera without microphone access?
q6.answer: Whistle detection requires microphone access. However, you can still use the app for regular photo and video capture without whistle detection. The microphone is only used for real-time whistle detection processing and no audio is recorded or stored.

q7.question: Can I use a timer instead of whistling?
q7.answer: Yes — countdown timer (3, 5, or 10 seconds) works with or without whistle detection enabled.

section4.title: 4. Datensicherheit
section4.content: Wir implementieren angemessene Sicherheitsmaßnahmen zum Schutz Ihrer persönlichen Informationen. Alle Fotos und Videos werden lokal auf Ihrem Gerät im privaten Verzeichnis der App gespeichert, auf das andere Apps nicht zugreifen können.

section6.content: Whistle Camera benötigt Zugriff auf die Kamera und das Mikrofon Ihres Geräts, um seine Kernfunktionalität bereitzustellen. Die Kamera wird für Foto- und Videoaufnahme verwendet. q1.question: What is Sudoku Fun Go?
q1.answer: Sudoku Fun Go is a classic 9×9 Sudoku game for iOS and Android. Fill every row, column, and 3×3 box with the digits 1–9 with no repeats. It offers four difficulty levels, notes mode, hints, undo, a timer, and persistent statistics including best times and scores per difficulty.

q2.question: Does the app work offline?
q2.answer: Yes. Gameplay, stats, and settings are stored locally. You can play fully offline. Internet is only needed for Game Center / Play Games leaderboards and achievements, and for optional in-app purchases (e.g. remove ads).

q3.question: What are the difficulty levels?
q3.answer: There are four levels: Easy, Medium, Hard, and Expert. Each affects the number of given clues and puzzle complexity. Best times and best scores are tracked separately per difficulty.

q4.question: Does the app support leaderboards?
q4.answer: Yes. On iOS we use Game Center with Friends and Global leaderboards for best score, plus achievements (e.g. first win, streak 5, streak 10). On Android we use Play Games Services with the same structure. Sign-in is prompted on app start.

q5.question: In which languages is the app available?
q5.answer: The app is localized in 12 base languages (English, Hebrew, Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Japanese, Korean, Chinese) with 17 regional variants. Hebrew and Arabic have full RTL support.

q6.question: How can I remove ads?
q6.answer: Sudoku Fun Go offers an in-app purchase to remove ads. After purchasing, ads are disabled. The purchase is restored automatically when you reinstall the app or use the same account on another device.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: No. Open the app and tap New Game. Game Center (iOS) or Google Play Games (Android) is optional for leaderboards and achievements.

items.question: Is the app free?
items.answer: Yes. Sudoku Fun Go is free to download and play. Ads support development. You can remove ads with a one-time in-app purchase.

items.question: Which devices are supported?
items.answer: iPhone and iPad (iOS), and Android phones and tablets with Google Play services. Requires a recent OS version — see the store listing for details.

items.question: What are the difficulty levels?
items.answer: | Level | For whom | |-------|----------| | Easy | Beginners — fewer empty cells | | Medium | Balanced daily challenge | | Hard | Experienced solvers | | Expert | Maximum empty cells — bravest players only |

items.question: Can I pause mid-game?
items.answer: Yes. Tap pause; the timer stops until you resume. Your puzzle is saved automatically.

items.question: What happens if I quit?
items.answer: If you leave without finishing, that puzzle’s progress is lost. Use Continue Game on the home screen to return to an in-progress puzzle.

items.question: How do notes work?
items.answer: Turn on Notes, tap a cell, then tap digits to mark candidates. When you place a confirmed number, matching notes in the row, column, and box can auto-clear (if enabled in Settings).

items.question: Do hints hurt my score?
items.answer: Yes. Each hint costs 300 points from your final score. Use them when stuck, but fewer hints = higher rank.

items.question: How many mistakes are allowed?
items.answer: Mistakes are tracked and reduce your score (−500 each). The puzzle does not end on a wrong entry — you can keep solving.

items.question: How is my score calculated?
items.answer: Base = 10,000 × difficulty multiplier (Easy ×1, Medium ×2, Hard ×3, Expert ×5) Minus 2 points per second, 500 per mistake, 300 per hint. Example: Expert, 10 minutes, 0 mistakes, 0 hints → up to 48,800 before time penalty details.

items.question: Why is my leaderboard empty?
items.answer: Sign in to Game Center or Play Games, complete a puzzle to submit a score, then open Statistics → Friends or Global. Install from the Play Store or App Store (not sideload) for full Play Games / billing support on Android.

items.question: How do achievements unlock?
items.answer: Automatically when you meet goals (first win, streaks, flawless solve, etc.). Open Statistics → Achievements to view progress.

items.question: What is Fun Facts! Trivia?
items.answer: A free mobile trivia game by Zuki Apps. Each round has 10 timed multiple-choice questions across eight fact categories, with scoring, streaks, stats, and optional leaderboards.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes. The app is free to download and play. Optional Premium removes ads with a one-time purchase (not a subscription).

items.question: iOS and Android?
items.answer: Yes — available on the App Store and Google Play.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: No account is required to play. Game Center (iOS) or Google Play Games (Android) is only needed for leaderboards and achievements.

items.question: How many questions per game?
items.answer: 10 questions per round.

items.question: What categories are available?
items.answer: Science & Nature · History & Culture · Geography & World · Odd Facts & Records · Movies & Film · Amazing Facts · Sports & Games · World Records.

items.question: What are the difficulty levels?
items.answer: - Easy — 20 seconds per question - Medium — 15 seconds - Hard — 12 seconds Harder modes increase score multipliers.

items.question: How does scoring work?
items.answer: Points come from base value × difficulty, plus time bonus, plus streak bonus (up to +50%), plus a +150 perfect-round bonus if you get 10/10. See the [user manual](../how-to/user_manual.md) for details.

items.question: What happens when time runs out?
items.answer: The question counts as incorrect and your streak resets.

items.question: Are questions the same every time?
items.answer: Questions are drawn from a large bundled bank (~395 per language) and shuffled so rounds feel fresh.

items.question: Can I play offline?
items.answer: Yes. Questions are stored in the app. Leaderboards, ads, and purchases need internet.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Hebrew, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Chinese (with regional variants where available).

items.title: 1. Protect your streak
items.description: Each correct answer in a row adds +10% to that answer’s score (max 5 → +50%). One wrong answer resets to zero. On harder questions, a streak is worth more than a risky guess.

items.title: 2. Don’t wait until the last second
items.description: Time bonus kicks in when you still have at least half the timer left. Tap confidently once you’re reasonably sure — dragging to 1 second leaves points on the table.

items.title: 3. Chase the perfect round
items.description: All 10 correct = +150 bonus on top of everything else. On Easy, learn the question style first; then push Medium or Hard for higher multipliers.

items.title: 4. Match difficulty to your goal
items.description: Easy — learn categories, build streak muscle memory - Medium — best balance for leaderboard pushes - Hard — ×2.0 multiplier; achievements like Hard Mode Win reward this ---

items.title: 5. Warm up with mixed Play
items.description: Home → Play mixes all categories. Great when you don’t know what you’re in the mood for and want variety.

items.title: 6. Grind weak categories
items.description: Check Stats for categories with lower accuracy. Dedicated category rounds improve both knowledge and mastery achievements.

items.title: 7. Read explanations
items.description: After answering, read the fact blurb when shown — many questions reuse themes or similar wording later.

items.title: 8. Use process of elimination
items.description: Four options every time. Cross off two obviously wrong choices before the timer gets tight. ---

q1.question: How do I create a custom workout?
q1.answer: To create a custom workout, tap the '+' button or 'New Workout' option. You can set the work duration, rest duration, number of rounds, prepare time, and cooldown period. Once created, your workout will be saved and can be used anytime.

q2.question: Can I use Power Interval Timer offline?
q2.answer: Yes! Power Interval Timer works completely offline. No internet connection is required. All your workouts and settings are stored locally on your device. The app does not collect any data or require an account.

q3.question: How do I cast the timer to my TV?
q3.answer: To cast to your TV, start a workout and look for the casting icon. On iOS, use AirPlay to cast to Apple TV or AirPlay-compatible devices. On Android, use MediaProjection to cast to Chromecast or other compatible devices. The timer will automatically optimize its layout for TV viewing.

q4.question: What display modes are available?
q4.answer: Power Interval Timer offers 7 different display modes: circular progress timer, digital countdown, linear progress bar, minimal clean design, analog clock style, dual timer display, and chronograph watch style. You can switch between modes in the settings or during a workout.

q5.question: How do I customize the timer appearance?
q5.answer: Go to Settings → Customization. You can customize timer images, color themes, font sizes, sound selection, and choose between Dark, Light, or System theme. Changes will be applied immediately to your timer display.

q7.question: What is the Premium upgrade?
q7.answer: Premium is a one-time $2.99 purchase that unlocks unlimited saved timers, all 10 display modes, and removes banner ads. The purchase is permanent and can be restored across devices using the same account.

q8.question: How do I tap to start/pause the timer?
q8.answer: Simply tap anywhere on the timer screen to start or pause your workout. This intuitive gesture control makes it easy to control your timer without looking for buttons, especially useful during intense workouts.

q9.question: Does Power Interval Timer support Apple Watch?
q9.answer: Yes! Power Interval Timer includes basic Apple Watch companion support. The watch app shows the current phase, countdown, and round number. Note that watch connectivity is disabled on TV devices.

q6.question: Does Power Interval Timer work on Apple TV and Android TV?
q6.answer: Yes! Power Interval Timer has native support for Apple TV (tvOS) and Android TV. You can install the app directly on your TV and use it with a remote control. The interface is optimized for large displays and includes platform-specific features.

section6.content: Power Interval Timer kann Zugriff auf die Fotobibliothek Ihres Geräts anfordern, damit Sie benutzerdefinierte Timer-Bilder auswählen können. Wir greifen nur auf Ihre Fotos zu, wenn Sie explizit wählen, ein Timer-Bild hinzuzufügen. Fotos werden lokal gespeichert und nicht an Dritte weitergegeben.

section7.title: 7. Absturzberichterstattung und Fehleranalyse
section7.content: Wir verwenden Firebase Crashlytics, einen von Google bereitgestellten Dienst, um Informationen über App-Abstürze und Fehler zu sammeln. Dies hilft uns, Probleme zu identifizieren und zu beheben, um Ihre Erfahrung zu verbessern.
dataCollectedItems: Technische Informationen über Abstürze und Fehler (Stack-Traces, Fehlermeldungen)
Installations-ID (eine eindeutige Kennung für Ihre App-Installation, nicht mit Ihrer persönlichen Identität verknüpft)
purposeItems: Fehler und Abstürze identifizieren und beheben
section7.dataSharingContent: Diese Daten werden von Google Firebase verarbeitet und unterliegen der Datenschutzrichtlinie von Google.
section7.dataRetentionContent: Absturzberichte werden von Firebase bis zu 90 Tage aufbewahrt und dann automatisch gelöscht.
section7.yourRightsContent: Sie können die Absturzberichterstattung deaktivieren, indem Sie sie in Ihren Geräteeinstellungen deaktivieren, obwohl dies uns daran hindern kann, Probleme zu beheben, die Sie betreffen.

items.title: Tabata & HIIT
items.description: Classic Tabata: 20 s on / 10 s off × 8 (4 min). Quality drops after round 6 — that’s normal; note it in your log. - HIIT blocks: 40/20 or 30/15 for 10–20 min total work time. - EMOM-style: set work = 60 s, rest = 0, rounds = minutes in block; start each minute on the beep.

items.title: Recovery & safety
items.description: If form breaks, pause — don’t chase the clock. - Hydrate during rest, not work. - Cooldown isn’t optional for high-intensity blocks with beginners.

items.title: Weekly habit

items.question: What is TempoLab Pro?
items.answer: A mobile app for practicing with your own audio files: change tempo and pitch, loop sections, add markers, use EQ/reverb/metronome, and export results. Built for musicians, singers, and anyone who learns by listening.

items.question: Which platforms are supported?
items.answer: iOS and Android. iPad and large Android tablets get an enhanced layout with side navigation.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes to download and practice. Ads appear in the free tier. A one-time Remove Ads purchase unlocks Premium features.

items.question: What languages are supported?
items.answer: 17 locales including English, Hebrew, Arabic, Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese (BR & PT), Russian, and Spanish.

items.question: What file formats can I import?
items.answer: Common formats such as MP3, M4A, WAV, and FLAC (device-dependent). If import fails, try another format or a non-DRM copy.

items.question: Why can’t I import my Apple Music / Spotify song?
items.answer: Streaming downloads are often DRM-protected. TempoLab Pro only works with files you have rights to use locally — recordings you made, purchased downloads without DRM, or stems shared with you.

items.question: Does changing tempo change pitch?
items.answer: Not by default. Tempo and pitch are independent. Vinyl mode links them intentionally for creative/record-style playback.

items.question: How precise is looping?
items.answer: You can nudge loop points by ±10 milliseconds — enough to align by ear to transients and grid.

items.question: What is formant correction?
items.answer: When shifting pitch on vocals, formant processing helps the voice keep a more natural character instead of sounding overly thin or heavy.

items.question: What’s the difference between waveform and spectrogram?
items.answer: Waveform shows amplitude over time; spectrogram shows frequency content. Both help you navigate — use whichever reads faster for your material.

items.question: Does Practice mode increase tempo automatically?
items.answer: Yes. You set start speed, end speed, and step size; the app ramps speed during the session.

items.question: Is there a metronome?
items.answer: Yes — use it alongside your track or with tap tempo to match BPM.

items.title: The 70% rule
items.description: If you can’t play it cleanly at 70% tempo, you’re not ready to speed up. Drop to 60%. Loop four bars, not the whole tune. TempoLab exists so you don’t waste reps on vanity speed. ---

items.title: Mark the money beats
items.description: Before you touch EQ or reverb, mark where the kick lands, where the vocal breathes, where the guitar slides. Your future self will jump there in one tap instead of scrubbing blind. ---

items.title: Loop small, win small
items.description: A two-bar loop beats a thirty-two-bar loop for muscle memory. Set A just before the downbeat, B on the last sixteenth you keep rushing. ±10 ms matters — your ear feels phase before your brain names it. ---

items.title: Pitch is for comfort, not cheating
items.description: Transpose down for morning warmups, up to test if a part still speaks when you’re tired. If you’re learning a cover, practice in the original key at least every third session so you don’t surprise yourself at the gig. ---

items.title: Vinyl mode has a vibe
items.description: When you link tempo and pitch, you get that sampler/record feel — useful for creative practice, not just transcription. Try it on a soul sample: slow down and hear the harmonics move together. ---

items.title: EQ while you practice, not after
items.description: Cut 200–400 Hz if the guitar mask is hiding the kick. Boost 3–5 kHz lightly to hear pick attack on acoustic parts. You’re not mastering — you’re making the part audible at low tempo. ---

items.title: Practice mode = honest reps
items.description: Set start 65%, end 95%, small steps. The day you fail is data: note the % and loop that bar again tomorrow. Speed is a side effect of accuracy. ---

items.title: Tap tempo before you argue BPM
items.description: Two producers, two numbers. Tap the groove — swing lies to the grid. Use metronome after you agree on feel. ---

q1.question: What number formats does Bit Scope support?
q1.answer: Bit Scope supports 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit integers, as well as Float16, Float32, and Float64 floating-point formats with full IEEE 754 compliance.

q2.question: Can I perform bitwise operations?
q2.answer: Yes! Bit Scope supports AND, OR, XOR, and NOT bitwise operations. You can use a second operand (B) to perform operations with your primary value (A).

q3.question: What number bases are supported?
q3.answer: Bit Scope supports Binary (BIN), Octal (OCT), Decimal (DEC), and Hexadecimal (HEX) number bases. You can input and display values in any of these bases with real-time conversion.

q4.question: Does Bit Scope work offline?
q4.answer: Yes! Bit Scope works completely offline. No internet connection is required. All calculations are performed locally on your device using BigInt for integers and IEEE 754 standards for floating-point numbers.

q5.question: What is the IEEE 754 breakdown feature?
q5.answer: The IEEE 754 breakdown provides a visual representation of floating-point number components, showing the sign bit, exponent, and mantissa separately. This is helpful for understanding how floating-point numbers are stored and represented.

q6.question: Can I change endianness and bit order?
q6.answer: Yes! Bit Scope allows you to switch between Big Endian (BE) and Little Endian (LE) byte ordering, as well as choose between MSB (Most Significant Bit) and LSB (Least Significant Bit) bit order for display.

items.question: What is Bit Scope?
items.answer: Bit Scope is a bit-level calculator and converter for developers. It converts between binary, octal, decimal, and hex; supports IEEE 754 floats; includes bitwise operations; and bundles converters and (with Premium) utilities like CRC and hash.

items.question: Is Bit Scope free?
items.answer: Yes. The calculator, bitwise tools, converters, history, and learning examples are free. Premium is a one-time purchase that removes ads and unlocks the Utilities tab.

items.question: Is Premium a subscription?
items.answer: No. Premium is a one-time in-app purchase. Future Premium-only tools are included without a recurring fee.

items.question: Which platforms are supported?
items.answer: iPhone and iPad (iOS). Android availability follows your Google Play listing. See store links on [zukiapps.com/bit-scope](https://zukiapps.com/bit-scope).

items.question: What languages are supported?
items.answer: 12 base languages with regional variants (e.g. en-US, pt-BR, zh-Hans). Hebrew and Arabic include full RTL layout.

items.question: How do I open the bit canvas?
items.answer: On the Calculator tab, open the bit grid preview or modal (tap the grid / bit visualization control). Tap any bit to toggle it; values update across all representations.

items.question: What is MSB vs LSB bit order?
items.answer: MSB lists bits from most significant to least. LSB reverses the visual order. Choose the view that matches your datasheet or protocol diagram.

items.question: What endianness settings do?
items.answer: Big-endian stores the most significant byte first; little-endian stores the least significant byte first. Bit Scope shows how your integer maps to bytes for each mode.

items.question: Can I use custom bit widths?
items.answer: Yes. Select a preset (8, 16, 32, 64) or enter a custom width (within app limits) for masks and registers that are not power-of-two aligned.

items.question: How does copy work?
items.answer: Tap the copy icon on a result card or tool output. Text is copied without extra spaces (e.g. binary groups may be stripped per field). A checkmark confirms success.

items.question: Which tools are free vs Premium?
items.answer: Free: Base64, color picker, timestamp, value comparator, batch converter—and all calculator features. Premium: CRC, hash, ASCII/Unicode viewer, QR generator, hex dump—and adding utility tools to custom widgets.

items.question: Why does CRC say input is too large?
items.answer: Very large inputs can exceed safe calculation limits on device. Shorten the input or split the data block. Bit Scope shows a clear message instead of failing silently.

items.title: Calculator
items.description: 1. Set width before paste — Pasting `0xFFFF` into 16-bit mode behaves differently than 32-bit. 2. Endian toggle for protocol bugs — Flip BE/LE when a register looks “byte-swapped”. 3. Gray code — Use for encoder / rotary sensor homework or validation. 4. Float switch — Jump INT → F32 to decode IEEE 754 from the same bit pattern. 5. History export — Snapshot a debug session before clearing the chip

items.title: Bit canvas
items.description: 6. Tap don't type — Mask tweaks (set bit 7) are faster on the grid than hex math. 7. Sign bit first — In signed 8-bit, bit 7 is your quick sanity check for negatives.

items.title: Copy workflow
items.description: 8. Checkmark = shipped — Wait for the icon before switching apps. 9. Paste once — Verify hex has no stray spaces before `gcc` or `xxd`.

items.title: Converters
items.description: 10. RAL colors — Firmware UI teams: match enclosure paint chips to RGB for docs. 11. Timestamp now — Tap current-time shortcut (if shown) for log correlation. 12. Comparator — Diff two hex constants side by side before merging branches.

items.title: Utilities (Premium)
items.description: 13. CRC test vectors — Keep `123456789` and `Hello` strings as mental benchmarks. 14. Hex dump import — Paste a snippet from `xxd` to inspect structure. 15. QR for Wi-Fi or URL — Field provisioning without a laptop.

items.title: Widgets & layout
items.description: 16. Three-tool board — Calculator tab + Widgets with Timestamp + Base64 covers 80% of bring-up. 17. iPad rail — Landscape uses side navigation—more vertical space for hex dump.

items.title: Settings
items.description: 18. Dark theme default — Easier on eyes during late firmware sessions; light for outdoor. 19. Language per device — System language vs in-app override for bilingual teams. 20. Learn tab — Send interns to Settings → Learn before asking about two's complement.

items.title: Premium
items.description: 21. One purchase — Restore on new phone before rebuying. 22. Future tools — Premium includes utilities we add later—no upsell tier. --- *Have a tip we should add? zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com*

items.question: What is Track Ledger?
items.answer: A simple GPS / GNSS track logger focused on data export and field use. It records your path as raw points with optional time- or distance-based sampling.

items.question: Is it a fitness or social app?
items.answer: No. There are no leaderboards, workouts, or social feeds. It's built for people who need reliable location logs and file exports.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: No. Everything is stored locally on your device.

items.question: Does it work offline?
items.answer: Yes. Recording and viewing saved tracks work without internet. The Map tab needs network to load OpenStreetMap tiles.

items.question: When does the app use my location?
items.answer: Only while you have an active recording (and optionally in the background if you have Premium and background recording enabled).

items.question: Why did recording pause when I switched apps?
items.answer: On the free plan, new GPS points are logged only while Track Ledger is in the foreground. Premium with background recording enabled continues logging when you leave the app.

items.question: What does "raw GNSS, no smoothing" mean?
items.answer: Points are saved as the device reports them. We don't apply a smoothing filter — you get the honest trace for your own analysis.

items.question: How many tracks can I save?
items.answer: Free: up to 10 completed tracks. Premium: unlimited (or a higher cap you choose in Settings).

items.question: What happens when the ledger is full?
items.answer: In Settings → Ledger you choose: automatically remove oldest tracks, or refuse new recordings until you delete tracks manually.

items.question: Which formats are free?
items.answer: CSV export and share are always available.

items.question: Which formats need Premium?
items.answer: GPX and GeoJSON.

items.question: Can I share a track to another app?
items.answer: Yes — use Share from track summary or Ledger. The system share sheet opens (Mail, Files, AirDrop, etc.).

items.title: GPS quality
items.description: Wait for a good fix before tapping Start — check GPS accuracy on the recording screen. - Open sky beats urban canyons and dense tree cover. - Raw points are logged without smoothing — if accuracy is poor, you'll see it in the data (by design).

items.title: Sampling strategy
items.description: | Scenario | Suggested setting | |----------|-------------------| | Walking / hiking | Time: every 5–10 s | | Driving / vehicle survey | Distance: every 25–50 m | | Long battery life | Wider intervals (time or distance) | | Detailed path capture | Tighter intervals (more points, larger files) |

items.title: Battery
items.description: Enable Keep screen on only when you need to watch the screen continuously. - Wider sampling intervals reduce CPU and GPS wakeups. - Background recording (Premium) uses more battery — expected for continuous logging.

items.title: Field workflow
items.description: 1. Create a project per job site or client. 2. Record with a consistent sampling preset per project type. 3. Export CSV in the field for quick checks; use GPX/GeoJSON back at the office. 4. Use Map tab to compare multiple passes on the same corridor.

items.title: Data hygiene
items.description: Rename tracks right after stop while context is fresh. - Delete test runs so you don't hit the free-tier ledger limit. - Premium users: set max completed tracks and when full policy before long field seasons.

items.title: Exports & GIS
items.description: CSV — columns include coordinates, time, elevation (as logged). Open in Excel, Python, R. - GPX — import to QGIS, Gaia GPS, or custom pipelines. - GeoJSON — drop into web maps or `ogr2ogr` workflows.

items.title: Premium worth it if you…
items.description: Need background logging while using camera or notes apps - Export to GPX or GeoJSON regularly - Want auto-stop at a fixed distance or duration - Record more than 10 completed tracks One-time purchase — no subscription.

items.question: What is Noise Meter — Shusher?
items.answer: A mobile app that estimates ambient sound level using your phone’s microphone, runs timed measurement sessions, scores the environment, saves history on device, and optionally alerts you in-app when noise exceeds a threshold (Shusher).

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes. Core metering, sessions, Shusher, and limited history are free. Premium is a one-time upgrade for charts, CSV export, longer sessions, themes, more history, and no ads.

items.question: iPhone or Android?
items.answer: Both. Bundle ID: `com.zuki.apps.noisemeter`.

items.question: What languages are supported?
items.answer: 12 locales including English, Hebrew, Arabic, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Portuguese, Russian, and Spanish. Change language in Settings.

items.question: How accurate is the decibel reading?
items.answer: It is an approximate level based on consumer phone hardware. Position, case, wind, and device model all affect results. Not calibrated for professional SPL, OSHA, or legal noise-ordinance enforcement.

items.question: Why does my reading differ from a real sound meter?
items.answer: Professional meters use calibrated microphones and specific measurement standards. Phones are tuned for speech and vary by manufacturer.

items.question: Can I use this in a dispute with a neighbor or landlord?
items.answer: Use readings as informal context only. For legal or compliance matters, hire a qualified acoustician with calibrated equipment.

items.question: Does the app record or upload audio?
items.answer: No continuous recording or cloud upload of raw audio. The app processes level data locally for metering and sessions.

items.question: When is the microphone active?
items.answer: While the app is open and metering or running a session—not as a background listener.

items.question: Why does iOS ask for tracking (ATT)?
items.answer: Free tier uses ads. Apple requires permission before personalized ad tracking. Premium removes ads.

items.question: Are Shusher alerts push notifications?
items.answer: No. They appear inside the app while the live meter is active.

items.question: Shusher doesn’t alert me
items.answer: Check: Shusher enabled in Settings · threshold not set too high · meter tab open and mic running · live level actually exceeds threshold.

items.title: Placement matters
items.description: 1. Same spot every time — compare “apples to apples” when tracking a room over days. 2. Mic unobstructed — remove thick cases; don’t cover the bottom edge (common mic location). 3. Orientation — pick portrait or landscape and stick with it; mic direction changes slightly. 4. Height — ear level for “how loud does it feel?”; desk level for workspace noise. 5. Distance — 0.5–1 m from the main noise s

items.title: When to use a short vs long session
items.description: | Goal | Suggestion | |------|------------| | Quick snapshot | 5 s (default) | | HVAC cycle | 15–30 s (Premium) | | Conversation level | 10–15 s while people talk naturally | | Street noise | 30–60 s (Premium) to catch peaks | ---

items.title: Simple average vs energy average (Leq)
items.description: Steady hum (fan, AC constant) → simple average is fine. - Bursty noise (dishes, traffic, classroom chatter) → switch to energy average (Leq) in Settings for a fairer score. ---

items.title: Shusher use cases
items.description: | Scenario | Starting threshold | |----------|-------------------| | Library / study | 45–55 dB | | Open office focus | 55–65 dB | | Living room with kids | 65–75 dB | | Workshop / garage | 75–85 dB | Lower the threshold if alerts rarely fire; raise it if too sensitive. ---

items.title: Compare before & after
items.description: 1. Measure before a change (rug, curtains, door seal, “quiet hours” sign). 2. Apply the change. 3. Measure after at the same time of day. 4. Save both to History with clear names. Small dB drops can mean a noticeably calmer space. ---

items.title: Sharing results without drama
items.description: Use neutral labels: “Tuesday 8pm apartment” not “neighbor noise proof.” - Share the results graphic for a single snapshot—not a live argument meter. - Remember: phone readings are approximate, not legal evidence. ---

items.title: Reduce false highs
items.description: Don’t speak directly into the mic while measuring ambient noise. - Move away from TV speakers and phone notifications. - Wind outdoors can spike readings—shield the mic or measure indoors. ---

items.title: Premium worth it?
items.description: Consider Premium if you: - Export CSV for a landlord, school, or internal report - Need session charts to see spikes during a meeting - Want longer samples and no ads during repeated daily checks One-time purchase—no subscription. ---

Some information may remain in encrypted backups for a limited time before automatic deletion, or where we must retain records by law.

childSafety.title: Child Safety Standards
childSafety.intro: ToldYa! is an invite-only social network operated by Zuki Apps. We maintain zero tolerance for child sexual abuse and exploitation (CSAE). This page describes our published standards, in-app reporting, moderation practices, and how to contact us regarding child safety.

section1.title: 1. Zero tolerance
section1.content: We prohibit any content or conduct that sexually exploits, endangers, or otherwise harms children. Accounts involved in CSAE may be removed immediately and permanently. Where required by law, we report to regional and national authorities and cooperate with law enforcement.

section2.title: 2. Standards against CSAE
section2.content: The following are prohibited on ToldYa! and may result in account termination and referral to authorities:
items: Child sexual abuse material (CSAM) in any form, including real, synthetic, or illustrated sexual content depicting minors
Sexualization of minors, grooming, or solicitation of minors
Content that facilitates exploitation or trafficking of children
Accounts that misrepresent age to contact or exploit minors
Links or instructions to obtain CSAM or to harm children
section2.content2: These rules are also reflected in our Terms of Use. We use a combination of automated signals, user reports, and human review to enforce them.

section3.title: 3. Age requirement
section3.content: ToldYa! is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age required in your country). Registration requires an invitation code and is intended for adults and teens who meet the minimum age. We may suspend accounts we believe belong to underage users.

section4.title: 4. In-app reporting
section4.content: Users can report child safety concerns in the app. Reporting options are available on predictions, comments, profiles, and messages (where the product provides a report or flag control). Reports are routed to our moderation queue and prioritized when they involve potential harm to minors.
section4.content2: When reporting, choose the category that best describes the issue and add any helpful context. You may also block users to prevent further contact.

section5.title: 5. Moderation & response
section5.content: We review child-safety reports as a priority. Confirmed CSAM is removed, preserving only what is legally required for evidence or mandatory reporting. Offending accounts are banned. We may preserve logs and content hashes as needed for compliance and cooperation with authorities.
section5.content2: We continually improve detection, reporting flows, and reviewer guidance. No system is perfect; we rely on our community and staff to identify harm quickly.

section6.title: 6. Legal compliance & authority reporting
section6.content: We comply with applicable child safety laws in the regions where we operate. Where appropriate, we report apparent CSAM to relevant organizations and authorities (for example, national hotlines or cybercrime units) in accordance with legal obligations and industry practice.
section6.content2: We do not use CSAE content for any purpose other than removal, mandatory reporting, and lawful cooperation with investigations.

section7.title: 7. Parental & guardian contact
section7.content: If you are a parent, guardian, or educator and believe a minor is using ToldYa! unsafely, contact us immediately using the email below with the username and a description of your concern. We will investigate and take appropriate action.

sectionContact.title: 8. Designated child safety contact
sectionContact.content: The designated point of contact for child safety, CSAM prevention practices, and compliance with Google Play child safety standards is ready to respond to inquiries from platforms, regulators, and users:
sectionContact.content2: For general app support (non–child-safety), you may also contact zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com. For privacy requests, see our Privacy Policy.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Zuki Apps (“we”, “us”) handles information when you use the Collagio mobile application and related pages at zukiapps.com/collagio (the “Service”). Collagio is a photo collage app that processes your images on your device. We do not require an account to use the core app.

section1.title: 1. Who we are
section1.content: The data controller for Collagio is Zuki Apps. Contact details are at the end of this Policy.
section1.content2: This Policy applies to the Collagio app (iOS and Android) and the legal pages we publish for the product. Third-party app stores have their own policies.

section2.title: 2. Information we collect
section2.content: Collagio is designed to minimize data collection. Depending on how you use the app, we may process:
items: Photos and videos you select from your device to create collages — processed locally on your device and not uploaded to our servers for core editing.
Collage projects and export history stored locally on your device (for example, recent collages kept in app history).
Optional in-app purchase status (for example, Premium / remove watermark) processed by Apple App Store or Google Play billing systems.
Support communications if you email us.
Limited diagnostic data if we enable crash or analytics tools in a future update; we will update this Policy before doing so.

section3.title: 3. How we use your information
items: Provide collage editing, layouts, filters, stickers, save, and share features
Remember your language preference and local settings
Process optional Premium purchases through the app stores
Improve stability and fix bugs when diagnostic tools are enabled
section3.content2: We do not sell your personal information. Collagio does not include third-party advertising in version 1.0.0.

section4.title: 4. Permissions
section4.content: The app may request access to your photo library or camera (if you choose to capture photos) solely to let you select images for collages. You can revoke permissions in your device settings. Collagio does not access your photos when you are not using those features.

section5.title: 5. Storage and retention
section5.content: Collages and edits are stored on your device. You can delete individual items from history or uninstall the app to remove local data. We do not operate a cloud account for Collagio in version 1.0.0.
section5.content2: If you contact support, we retain correspondence as long as needed to resolve your request and for reasonable business records.

section6.title: 6. Purchases
section6.content: Optional Premium features (such as removing the export watermark) are sold as a one-time in-app purchase through Apple or Google. Payment and purchase history are handled by the store; we receive only what the store provides to confirm entitlement.

section7.title: 7. Sharing with third parties
section7.content: We do not share your photos with third parties for marketing. When you use the system share sheet, exports go to apps you choose (Messages, WhatsApp, Files, etc.) under their policies.
section7.content2: We use Apple and Google only as needed for distribution, billing, and platform services.

section8.title: 8. Your rights and children
section8.content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or object to certain processing. Contact us to exercise rights. We may need to verify your identity.
section8.content2: Collagio is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age in your country). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.

section9.title: 9. Changes
section9.content: We may update this Policy from time to time. We will post the revised version with a new “Last updated” date. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy.
